festivals.name: Zanzibar Festival of the Dhow Countries
festivals.description: East Africa's premier arts festival brings music, film, and culture from across the Indian Ocean dhow world to Stone Town.

festivals.name: Sauti za Busara
festivals.description: East Africa's most beloved African music festival showcases emerging and established artists in Zanzibar's Stone Town Fort.

destinations.name: Serengeti National Park
destinations.description: The world's greatest wildlife spectacle: 1.5 million wildebeest migrate across this vast savannah with lions, leopards, and elephants.

destinations.name: Zanzibar
destinations.description: The Spice Island has a UNESCO Stone Town, white-sand beaches, and a complex history as East Africa's most important trade hub.

destinations.name: Ngorongoro Crater
destinations.description: The world's largest inactive volcanic caldera is a natural enclosure with the highest density of big game in Africa.

destinations.name: Mount Kilimanjaro
destinations.description: Africa's highest peak at 5,895m is the world's tallest free-standing mountain, accessible via non-technical trekking routes.

destinations.name: Selous / Nyerere National Park
destinations.description: Africa's largest UNESCO reserve has boat safaris, walking safaris, and excellent African wild dog sightings.

destinations.name: Lake Manyara
destinations.description: A soda lake famous for millions of flamingos and the unusual tree-climbing lions at the base of the Great Rift Valley.

destinations.name: Pemba Island
destinations.description: The Green Island has the best diving in East Africa thanks to dramatic coral walls, with the world's largest clove production.

destinations.name: Ruaha National Park
destinations.description: Tanzania's largest national park is off the tourist trail with huge elephant herds and exceptional, authentic safari experiences.
